_comment: Canonical wording for the demo's preset-scenario buttons. The frontend ScenarioPanel hardcodes the same messages (it does not read this file at runtime), and the backend doc-sync test (tests/test_doc_sync.py) pins the two copies byte-for-byte so they cannot drift. Messages are a real user's words (the why/when of the task) — never tool mechanics.

scenarios.label: Deep research
scenarios.message: I need a thorough, fact-checked answer on the main trade-offs between retrieval-augmented generation and long-context LLMs. Please dig into it from several angles, cross-check the findings, and give me a clear writeup.

scenarios.label: Pick it back up
scenarios.message: Earlier you started looking into that research question for me. Can you pick it back up where you left off rather than starting over from scratch?

scenarios.label: Novel task
scenarios.message: There's no standard playbook for this one, so I'd like you to work out a procedure yourself: research a few topics, refine them, have skeptics challenge each finding, and then synthesize what survives.

scenarios.label: Delegate heavy work
scenarios.message: This is a heavy, multi-step research job — I don't want to babysit every step. Please take it off my hands and run the whole thing in the background; just let me know how it went once it's done.

scenarios.label: Make it pass
scenarios.hint: fix code until the tests are green
scenarios.message: I've got a small TypeScript module with a couple of failing unit tests. Please actually fix the code and keep checking it against the tests until they genuinely pass — don't just tell me it looks right, prove it builds and the tests go green.

scenarios.label: Sign off mid-run
scenarios.message: Before you actually run the staging deployment, I want to sign off on the plan myself — walk me through the riskiest steps and pause for my approval before you proceed.

scenarios.label: Refactor swarm
scenarios.hint: parallel fixes merged into a PR
scenarios.message: There are a few separate bugs in this little module I'd like fixed all at once. Please have several helpers each take a fix in parallel, review the patches, merge them together into one change — sorting out any conflicts — and open a pull request with the result.
